title: Signals
summary: Launch a long-running script and stop it with SIGTERM.

steps.explanation: Background jobs keep running while you return to the shell prompt.
SIGTERM asks a process to shut down gracefully before harder signals.
steps.hint: Use kill -15 or kill -TERM with the hang.sh PID.

steps.prompt: Confirm hang.sh is no longer running.
steps.explanation: Re-running ps or pgrep shows whether the process survived the signal.
steps.hint: Use ps or pgrep — you should not see hang.sh running.
